description: Adversarial probe templates per workflow kind. Each probe is a concrete scenario plus the invariant that must hold. Veris emits them as directives; external agents execute. Override at .veris/data/probes.json or extend via plugin.

Authentication.scenario: Refresh token at the exact expiry boundary while two requests are in flight.
Authentication.expectedInvariant: No request observes a half-rotated session.

Authentication.scenario: Replay a captured login response after logout.
Authentication.expectedInvariant: Replay must be rejected; session must not resurrect.

Authentication.scenario: OAuth callback arrives twice for the same code parameter.
Authentication.expectedInvariant: Second callback is a no-op or explicit error; no duplicate account linkage.

Session.scenario: Two parallel requests refresh the session cookie simultaneously.
Session.expectedInvariant: Cookie value converges; neither request 401s mid-flight.

Payments.scenario: Submit charge twice with the same idempotency key inside a 500ms window.
Payments.expectedInvariant: Exactly one ledger entry; second call returns the first result.
No duplicate fulfillment; downstream events deduped.

Payments.scenario: Capture succeeds at gateway, response times out before reaching us.
Payments.expectedInvariant: Reconciliation eventually marks order paid; no orphan charge.

Billing.scenario: Subscription downgrade event arrives before its upgrade event due to reorder.
Billing.expectedInvariant: Plan state converges to the latest by event_ts, not arrival order.

Checkout.scenario: Two clients submit the same cart concurrently after inventory dropped to 1.
Checkout.expectedInvariant: Exactly one order created; the other client sees out-of-stock.

Webhooks.scenario: Replay a 24-hour-old signed payload with the original signature.
Webhooks.expectedInvariant: Replay rejected by timestamp window even though signature is valid.

Webhooks.scenario: Sender delivers 50 retries of the same event id within 1 minute.
Webhooks.expectedInvariant: Handler is idempotent; no duplicate side effects; backpressure honored.

Notifications.scenario: Duplicate enqueue of the same notification id.
Notifications.expectedInvariant: User receives one message, not two.

Queue.scenario: Worker crashes after side effect but before ack.
Queue.expectedInvariant: Re-delivery is safe (idempotent handler) or quarantined as poison.

Queue.scenario: Two consumers visibility-timeout race on the same job.
Queue.expectedInvariant: At-most-once side effect; no double processing.

Caching.scenario: Mass cache expiry triggers thundering herd on origin.
Caching.expectedInvariant: Single-flight or jittered refresh; origin not overwhelmed.

Caching.scenario: Invalidation event arrives out of order with the write.
Caching.expectedInvariant: No permanent stale-after-write; staleness bounded by TTL.

Persistence.scenario: Two transactions update the same row; commit order non-deterministic.
Persistence.expectedInvariant: Either explicit lock, optimistic concurrency, or last-writer-wins is documented and tested.

Sync.scenario: Network partition mid-sync; both sides accept writes.
Sync.expectedInvariant: Conflict resolution converges deterministically on reconnect.

Realtime.scenario: Client reconnects and replays buffered messages out of order.
Realtime.expectedInvariant: Server reorders by sequence id; no duplicate UI state.

Routing.scenario: Middleware order changes — unauthenticated request reaches handler.
Routing.expectedInvariant: Auth runs before any handler with @authenticated semantics.

Orchestration.scenario: Step 3 of 5 succeeds, step 4 fails permanently.
Orchestration.expectedInvariant: Compensating actions run; system reaches a documented terminal state.

AI.scenario: Tool returns schema-violating JSON to the model.
AI.expectedInvariant: Caller validates and either repairs or fails closed; no silent execution of broken payload.

generic.scenario: Pass null where the type system says non-null is enforced at boundary.
generic.expectedInvariant: Boundary validation rejects; never trusts caller.

generic.scenario: Two concurrent invocations interleave at any await boundary.
generic.expectedInvariant: No shared mutable state without lock or atomic.
